dyscrasias.name: Bully
dyscrasias.effect: Gain a +1 bonus to your offensive test pool in a single physical or social challenge vs. a character with fewer or equal dots of Status.

dyscrasias.name: Envy
dyscrasias.effect: Gain a +1 bonus to your offensive test pool in a single physical or social challenge vs. a character with greater than or equal your dots of Status.

dyscrasias.name: Principles
dyscrasias.effect: Gain a +1 bonus to your defensive test pool in a single mental or social challenge vs. an ideological enemy. (Usually , this “enemy” is a vampire from a different sect.)

dyscrasias.name: Vengeful
dyscrasias.effect: Gain a +1 bonus in a single challenge vs. a character if you have lost a previous challenge to her in the same night.

dyscrasias.name: Vicious
dyscrasias.effect: Gain a +1 bonus in a single challenge against a character if you have won a previous challenge against her in the same night.

dyscrasias.name: Driving
dyscrasias.effect: You may spend this Dyscrasia instead of one Rouse check to activate a single Physical (Celerity, Potence, or Fortitude) power. If the power you are attempting to activate requires multiple Rouse checks you make one less.

dyscrasias.name: In Mourning
dyscrasias.effect: You may spend this Dyscrasia instead of one Rouse check to activate a single Social power (Animalism, Dominate, Presence). If the power you are attempting to activate requires multiple Rouse checks, you make one less.

dyscrasias.name: Lost Love
dyscrasias.effect: Gain a +1 bonus to any single offensive challenge involving protecting and preserving any of your Touchstones.

dyscrasias.name: Lost Relative
dyscrasias.effect: Gain a +1 bonus to any single defensive challenge involving protecting and preserving any of your Touchstones.

dyscrasias.name: Massive Failure
dyscrasias.effect: Gain a +1 bonus to your defensive test pool in a single challenge against a power that you have failed to resist at least one time before during the same night.

dyscrasias.name: Nostalgic
dyscrasias.effect: Gain a +1 bonus to your offensive test pool in a single challenge using a power that you have successfully used at least one time before during the same night.

dyscrasias.name: Recalling
dyscrasias.effect: Gain a +1 bonus to your defensive test pool in a single challenge against a power that you have seen successfully used at least one time before during the same night.

dyscrasias.name: Chill
dyscrasias.effect: You may spend this Dyscrasia to gain a +1 bonus to resist Fury Frenzy.

dyscrasias.name: Comfortably Numb
dyscrasias.effect: You may spend this Dyscrasia to gain a +2 bonus to resist Fear Frenzy.

dyscrasias.name: Eating Your Emotions
dyscrasias.effect: You may spend this Dyscrasia to gain a +1 bonus to resist Hunger Frenzy.

dyscrasias.name: Given Up
dyscrasias.effect: Gain a +1 bonus to your defensive test pool on your current challenge if another character has achieved a critical win against you at least one time before during the same night.

dyscrasias.name: Lone Wolf
dyscrasias.effect: Gain a +1 bonus to your test pool on a single challenge if you have no allies present.

dyscrasias.name: Procrastinate
dyscrasias.effect: Gain a +1 bonus to your test pool on a single challenge if you are the last person to act during the current turn of a combat scenario.

dyscrasias.name: Reflection
dyscrasias.effect: You may spend this Dyscrasia instead of one Rouse check to activate a single Mental power (Auspex, Obfuscate, and most Thin-Blood Alchemy, Blood Sorcery and Oblivion powers, including Rituals and Ceremonies). If the power you are attempting to activate requires multiple Rouse checks, you make one less.

dyscrasias.name: Enduring Enthusiasm
dyscrasias.effect: You may spend this Dyscrasia instead of making a Rouse check to Mend.

dyscrasias.name: Smell Game
dyscrasias.effect: You may spend this Dyscrasia the next time you feed. Take an additional Dyscrasia during that feeding, then choose which one to keep. This is an exception to the rule that states you cannot benefit from more than one Dyscrasia in a single game.

dyscrasias.name: High on Life
dyscrasias.effect: You may spend this Dyscrasia instead of making a Rouse check to appear lifelike, giving you a beating heart, a normal coloration, and a passable autonomic system. You will appear lifelike for one hour. This benefit immediately ends if you feed.

dyscrasias.name: Manic High
dyscrasias.effect: Gain a +1 bonus to your test pool if your last successful challenge was a critical win.

dyscrasias.name: True Love
dyscrasias.effect: You may spend this Dyscrasia to gain a +1 bonus to your defensive test pool for a single challenge when using Assist Defender.

dyscrasias.name: Stirring
dyscrasias.effect: You may spend this Dyscrasia instead of making a Rouse check to Blood Surge.
